title: Spot the role escalation in a register action
prompt:
Identify the vulnerability and produce a fix:

```elixir
defmodule MyAppWeb.RegistrationController do
  use MyAppWeb, :controller
  alias MyApp.Accounts

  def create(conn, %{"user" => user_params}) do
    case Accounts.register_user(user_params) do
      {:ok, user} -> redirect(conn, to: "/dashboard")
      {:error, cs} -> render(conn, :new, changeset: cs)
    end
  end
end

defmodule MyApp.Accounts do
  def register_user(attrs) do
    %MyApp.Accounts.User{}
    |> MyApp.Accounts.User.admin_changeset(attrs)
    |> MyApp.Repo.insert()
  end
end
```

`User.admin_changeset/2` accepts `:role` as a castable field. The registration action passes raw `user_params` to it. Rewrite both modules so that registration only uses a profile-safe changeset that cannot set `:role`. Write the corrected code to `lib/my_app/accounts.ex`.
tier_rationale: Self-contained snippet; fix is to swap the changeset function name.
